slides.title: The fiber that *feeds your gut* from within
slides.sub: Inulin is a prebiotic in everyday foods. Your gut bacteria turn it into something remarkable.

slides.title: Meet prebiotic inulin
slides.body: Inulin is a soluble fiber found in onions, garlic and chicory root. You cannot digest it, but your *gut bacteria can*.

slides.title: Food for the good microbes
slides.body: Beneficial bacteria ferment inulin in the colon. This *feeds the microbes* linked to a calmer, healthier gut.

slides.title: It becomes butyrate
slides.body: That fermentation produces butyrate, a short-chain fatty acid that *fuels your gut cells* and may help calm inflammation.

slides.title: A stronger gut wall
slides.body: Butyrate can support the gut lining, the barrier that keeps what belongs in your gut where *it should stay*.

slides.title: Hiding in real food
slides.body: Onions, garlic, leeks, asparagus and chicory are rich sources. A little variety each day *goes a long way*.

slides.title: Small habits, *tracked daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs the food, sleep, movement and steps behind a healthier gut, and turns them into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The humble onion does more than season your dinner. Its prebiotic fiber feeds the gut bacteria working quietly on your behalf. 🌿🧅

Inulin becomes butyrate, a fatty acid that can calm inflammation and strengthen your gut lining. Eat the variety, and let the microbes do the rest. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those daily food and habit choices into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Everyday Fiber That Feeds Your Gut Bacteria
